summary: Verified nav & footer logo bug-fix on Aurrum Intelligence landing. Both images now load correctly (naturalWidth > 0, complete=true, visibility=visible, opacity=1) and no failed image requests. Navbar logo renders correctly and the AURRUM INTELLIGENCE wordmark is clearly legible in the top-left. Footer logo LOADS but is severely CROPPED because a 5:1 wide banner PNG (800x160) is forced into a 144x144 square with `object-cover`, so the tagline 'READ THE MARKET. TRADE THE EDGE.' is NOT legible — fails the visual verification requirement of BUG FIX 2.

ui_bugs.issue: Footer logo image (natural 800x160, ~5:1 aspect) is placed in a square 144x144 container with `object-cover object-center`, which crops away most of the wordmark and the entire 'READ THE MARKET. TRADE THE EDGE.' tagline. Only partial letters 'RRU / ELLIGEN' are visible. Fails the requirement that the lockup + tagline be legible.

issues: Logo container should be a rectangle matching the image aspect ratio (e.g. w-44 h-9 / w-52 h-11) OR the class should switch from `object-cover` to `object-contain` so the whole lockup is visible.

Optional: user described the mark as 'inside a black block', but the PNGs have transparent/light backgrounds — the mark renders on the cream page background, not on a black block. If a black block backing was intended, wrap the in a black div.

nav_logo.spec_pass: PASS — element exists, image loaded (naturalWidth>0), top<30, left<20, width>=200, height>=60, wordmark visually legible in screenshot

footer_logo.spec_pass: PARTIAL — image loaded and size 144x144 is within the requested 120-160 range, BUT the visual-legibility requirement fails because object-cover crops a 5:1 image into a 1:1 box and the tagline is not visible.

verification_details.network: No failed requests. Both logo URLs returned successfully.

action_items: In /app/frontend/src/components/site/Footer.jsx line 15, either (a) change `object-cover` to `object-contain` and widen the container to match aspect (e.g. `w-40 sm:w-48 h-auto`), or (b) keep it small but use a square version of the lockup asset. Verify tagline 'READ THE MARKET. TRADE THE EDGE.' is legible.
Optional: if a black block backdrop is desired (per user's description), wrap the in a `bg-black p-3` container.
critical_code_review_comments: Footer.jsx uses `object-cover` on a non-square image forced into a square — this pattern will always crop; use `object-contain` when preserving the full mark is required.

Navbar logo also uses `object-cover object-center` on `h-16 sm:h-20 w-52 sm:w-64` — the aspect (256:80 = 3.2:1) is close to the image aspect (1200:480 = 2.5:1) so cropping is mild and the wordmark is still legible, but object-contain would be safer.

success_rate.frontend: 66% (nav pass, footer size-pass but visual-fail, regression pass)

context_for_next_testing_agent: The bug is purely CSS on Footer.jsx line 15: object-cover on a square container crops the wide lockup. Fix by switching to object-contain and/or a rectangular container. Nav logo is fine. No backend testing needed.
